text: Being here, at the threshold and then within a thoughtfully designed space dedicated to research and development, I am struck by a sense of curiosity and anticipation. The glass walls create a visible barrier between the outside world and the vibrant, immersive exhibit inside. Upon entering, I am greeted by bold colors, dynamic patterns, and the glowing presence of screens and text that invite contemplation. I feel like both an observer and a participant in a play of light, texture, and ideas. The crafted environment encourages introspection; I am aware of the artifice but also drawn into its meditative possibilities. There is a stillness here, tinged with excitement—the promise of discovery interwoven with the subtle anxiety of being watched or catalogued by the technologies on display. I feel thoughtful, slightly detached, and also strangely comforted by the intentionality of the space.
